## Objective

Teach **crash-dump triage** with Copilot using BUG-001 (arena OOB write). The session is
60 minutes tight; BUG-003 is a stretch goal documented as "skip if behind".

## Context references

- `#file:../instructions/facilitator-script.instructions.md`
- `#file:../../factory-overlay/demo-patterns/crash-dump-triage.md`
- `#file:../../output/ea-cpp-games/fixtures/crash_dumps/expected/BUG-001.md`
- `#file:../../output/ea-cpp-games/fixtures/seeded-bugs.md`

## Pre-conditions

- Crash-dump fixture (BUG-001) generated by facilitator within 24h of session.
- engine_demo builds in Debug.

## Session shape (60 ±3 min)

| Block         | Time        | Activities                                                                                                         |
| ------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Recap + intro | 00:00–05:00 | Recap S1 context belt; preview today's fault.                                                                      |
| Concept       | 05:00–12:00 | Minidump anatomy, PDB-source linkage, Copilot's role.                                                              |
| Demo: BUG-001 | 12:00–38:00 | Load .dmp; ask Copilot to identify faulting frame; produce root cause; propose fix; write regression test; re-run. |
| Hands-on      | 38:00–52:00 | Exercises 01 + 02 against a fresh repro.                                                                           |
| Stretch / Q&A | 52:00–58:00 | If time: BUG-003 (UAF). Otherwise: discuss false-positive triage.                                                  |
| Survey        | 58:00–60:00 | Retro.                                                                                                             |

## Steps

Apply the same shape as Session 1's prompt. Specific facilitation notes:

- The script begins with the facilitator running `run-and-capture.sh` LIVE (or showing the
  pre-captured dump) to demonstrate fixture provenance.
- The Copilot prompt to use first: _"Open the attached minidump. Identify the faulting
  frame and the most likely root-cause hypothesis. Cite specific source lines from
  engine_demo."_ Verbatim in `text` fence.
- `Watch for:` callout — Copilot may attribute the fault to EASTL internals; the facilitator
  redirects to engine_demo.
- HITL gate at 38:00 before applying the fix: review the proposed patch.

`exercises/01-triage-bug001.md` mirrors the demo with a different repro program.
`exercises/02-write-the-fix-test.md` asks the learner to produce a GoogleTest that fails on
the bugged version and passes on the fixed version.

## Output contract

7 files; script totals 60 ±3 min; BUG-001 cited correctly; BUG-003 marked as stretch with a
clear "skip if behind" callout.

## Self-validation

```bash
npm run lint
grep -q "BUG-001" sessions/02-crash-dumps/facilitator-script.md
grep -q "stretch" sessions/02-crash-dumps/facilitator-script.md
```

## Failure modes

- **Demo dependent on a binary that isn't reproducible**: the facilitator must show the
  capture script even if they don't run it live, to maintain the provenance story.
- **Copilot hallucination accepted silently**: every claim Copilot makes about the dump must
  be verified against the source/disassembly during the demo.
